title: JSX fundamentals
description: What JSX compiles to and its gotchas.

options: React.createElement calls (or jsx() with the new transform)
questions.explanation: JSX is syntactic sugar. Browsers never see JSX — bundlers transform it.

questions.prompt: Why do we write className instead of class?
options: class is a reserved word in JavaScript
questions.explanation: class collides with the ES class keyword, so JSX renamed it.

options: No — a component must return a single root (use fragment or wrapper)

questions.explanation: Both work. Use <> when you don't need to pass a key.
false, null, and undefined render as nothing in JSX.

options: 0 shows in the div — classic gotcha
questions.explanation: 0 is falsy but JSX renders it as literal 0. Use {count > 0 && ...} instead.
Anything in {} is a JS expression evaluated at render.
